---
title: "surface_dice"
generated: true
description: "Normalised surface Dice at a boundary `tolerance`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.metrics.surface`](../nitrix-metrics-surface.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:surface_dice -->
```python
surface_dice(pred: Bool[Array, '*spatial'], target: Bool[Array, '*spatial'], *, tolerance: float, spacing: _Spacing = None, backend: Backend = 'auto') -> Float[Array, '']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:surface_dice -->
Normalised surface Dice at a boundary `tolerance`.

The fraction of both surfaces lying within `tolerance` of the other:
$(|\{S_{\text{pred}} : d \le \tau\}| +
|\{S_{\text{target}} : d \le \tau\}|) /
(|S_{\text{pred}}| + |S_{\text{target}}|)$.  This is the count form of the
metric (surface voxels counted, not areas), following MONAI's
`compute_surface_dice` with `use_subvoxels=False`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:surface_dice -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `pred` | `Bool` | `*spatial` | — | Binary masks (any non-zero is foreground), of the same spatial shape. |
| `target` | `Bool` | `*spatial` | — | Binary masks (any non-zero is foreground), of the same spatial shape. |
| `tolerance` | `float` | — | — | Boundary tolerance $\tau$ in `spacing` units. |
| `spacing` | `_Spacing` | — | `None` | Per-axis voxel size (scalar or one per axis); `None` uses unit spacing. |
| `backend` | `Backend` | — | `'auto'` | Execution engine for the underlying [`erode`](../nitrix-morphology/erode.qmd) and Euclidean distance transform. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape —. Scalar normalised surface Dice in $[0, 1]$; `1` for identical masks, `0` when a class is present in only one mask, and `nan` when both surfaces are empty (matching MONAI). Non-differentiable (a reporting metric).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:surface_dice -->
### References

Nikolov S, Blackwell S, Zverovitch A, et al. (2018). Deep learning to
achieve clinically applicable segmentation of head and neck anatomy for
radiotherapy. arXiv:1809.04430. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.1809.04430
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:surface_dice -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
